name: Emperor's Castle
summary: Imperial-scale residence, governance, archive, treasury, ceremonial, and military coordination complex for the Principalities.
day_to_day: Processes petitions, tribute intake, inter-house diplomacy, legal review, intelligence briefings, military dispatches, and heavily managed ceremonial hosting.
who_works_there: Stewards, archivists, guards, tribute assessors, ceremonial overseers, military officers, servants, and court households.
who_uses_it: The Emperor, court nobility, house envoys, tribute carriers, petitioners, and selected military or legal officers.
authority: Imperial mediation, law, tribute, intelligence, and symbolic unity within the Principalities.
public_manifestation: Throne chambers, council rooms, restricted archives, vaults, barracks, guest suites, ritual halls, and controlled inner courts.
conflict_or_bottleneck: Inter-house rivalry, tribute disputes, intelligence leakage, and managed access pressure make the castle politically volatile even in peacetime.
